steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage says Ware's compositions can be ‘explained in as many ways as there are people to listen to them.’ The blank must name something that can exist in multiple valid forms for the same piece.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'performances': refers to live renderings of the music, not different understandings of its meaning. ✗
  • Choice B — 'revisions': refers to edited versions of the work — not how listeners understand it. ✗
  • Choice C — 'influences': refers to sources that shaped the composition — unrelated to listener understanding. ✗
  • Choice D — 'interpretations': denotes multiple valid ways of understanding the same piece — matches the clue about being explained differently by each listener. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
